pattern: Scanning left to right and popping any earlier digit that is larger than the current one greedily removes the most significant high digits first, which is exactly what minimizes the resulting number; a monotonic stack realizes this in one pass.
hints: To make the number small, you want the leftmost digits as small as possible, so a larger digit immediately followed by a smaller one should be dropped.
Keep a stack of kept digits; before pushing a new digit, pop the top while it is larger than the new digit and you still have removals left.
If removals remain after the pass, trim them from the end; finally strip leading zeros and treat an empty result as the string zero.

edge_inputs.description: remove three to leave the smallest
removing a leading digit exposes leading zeros that must be stripped
already ascending, so removals come from the end
strictly descending, so removals come from the front
interior zeros pulled to the front collapse to a single zero
single digit removed entirely is zero

stress.description: long random digit strings with a random removal count never exceeding the length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    digits = [rng.choice('0123456789') for _ in range(n)]
    if digits[0] == '0' and n > 1:
        digits[0] = rng.choice('123456789')
    num = ''.join(digits)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [num, k]
stress.note: k ranges over the whole valid span so both pop-driven and tail-trim paths trigger
